questions.q: In the reaction 2Na (s) + Cl2 (g) → 2NaCl (s), the oxidation state of sodium changes from
questions.explanation: Sodium is oxidized from 0 (in metallic state) to +1 (in ionic state).

questions.q: The oxidation number of chlorine in HClO4 is
questions.explanation: Chlorine has a higher oxidation state in HClO4 due to the presence of four oxygen atoms.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of disproportionation reaction?
questions.explanation: Disproportionation reaction involves the oxidation and reduction of the same element.

questions.q: What is the type of reaction that involves the transfer of electrons between two substances?
questions.explanation: Redox reactions involve the transfer of electrons, which leads to a change in oxidation states.

questions.q: The oxidation state of manganese in KMnO4 is
questions.explanation: Manganese has a higher oxidation state in KMnO4 due to the presence of four oxygen atoms.

questions.q: Identify the oxidizing agent in the reaction 2MnSO4 + K2Cr2O7 → K2SO4 + 2Cr2(SO4)3 + Mn2(SO4)3?
questions.topic: Redox Reactions - Oxidizing and Reducing Agents

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a redox reaction with oxygen?
questions.explanation: Redox reactions with oxygen involve oxidation.

questions.q: In the reaction 2KMnO4 + 3H2SO4 → K2SO4 + 2MnSO4 + 3H2O + 5[O] , how many electrons are transferred?
questions.explanation: The number of electrons transferred is equal to the number of oxygen atoms produced.

questions.q: What is the change in oxidation state of chlorine in the reaction 2Na (s) + Cl2 (g) → 2NaCl (s)?
questions.explanation: Chlorine is oxidized from 0 (in elemental state) to +1 (in ionic state).

questions.q: Identify the reducing agent in the reaction Al (s) + CuSO4 (aq) → Al2(SO4)3 (aq) + Cu (s)?
questions.topic: Redox Reactions - Oxidizing and Reducing Agents

questions.q: The oxidation number of manganese in Mn2O7 is
questions.explanation: Manganese has a higher oxidation state in Mn2O7 due to the presence of seven oxygen atoms.

questions.q: What is the difference between oxidation and reduction in redox reactions?
opts: (A) Oxidation involves gain of electrons, while reduction involves loss of electrons
(B) Oxidation involves loss of electrons, while reduction involves gain of electrons
(C) Oxidation involves gain of protons, while reduction involves loss of protons
(D) Oxidation involves loss of protons, while reduction involves gain of protons
questions.explanation: Oxidation involves loss of electrons, while reduction involves gain of electrons.

questions.q: The oxidation number of copper in CuSO4 is
questions.explanation: Copper has an oxidation state of +2 in CuSO4.

questions.q: Identify the oxidizing agent in the reaction 2KMnO4 + 3H2SO4 → K2SO4 + 2MnSO4 + 3H2O + 5[O]?
questions.topic: Redox Reactions - Oxidizing and Reducing Agents

questions.q: What is the type of reaction that involves the transfer of electrons between two substances, resulting in a change in oxidation states?
questions.explanation: Redox reactions involve the transfer of electrons, which leads to a change in oxidation states.

questions.q: The oxidation number of chlorine in HClO is
questions.explanation: Chlorine has a higher oxidation state in HClO due to the presence of oxygen.

questions.q: What is the change in oxidation state of iron in the reaction Fe2O3 + 3CO → 2Fe + 3CO2?
questions.explanation: Iron is reduced from +3 (in Fe2O3) to 0 (in Fe).

questions.q: Identify the reducing agent in the reaction 2KMnO4 + 5SO2 + H2O → K2SO4 + 2MnSO4 + 2H2SO4 + 2H2O?
questions.topic: Redox Reactions - Oxidizing and Reducing Agents

questions.q: The oxidation number of manganese in KMnO4 is related to?
opts: (D) The number of protons in manganese
questions.explanation: The oxidation number of manganese in KMnO4 is related to the number of oxygen atoms.
